definition: Trifurcation provides false comfort during correlated macro shocks. While supply elasticities genuinely differ (trifurcation holds structurally), common demand shocks re-correlate all three sub-complexes. Trifurcation is a supply-side framework being misapplied to demand-side questions.
evidence: March 19, 2026 sell-off: Iran-driven oil spike simultaneously pushed gold down (via real rates) and copper down (via recession fears)
All three 'independent' sub-complexes moved together through a common macro channel
Resolved as boundary condition: trifurcation holds structurally but fails for risk management during tail events
open_questions: How frequently do correlated demand shocks override supply-side trifurcation?
Can portfolio construction account for both steady-state trifurcation and tail-event re-correlation?
